Utopia



Utopia.

Indeed, it is a powerful concept. One that authors and photographers alike use to evoke human emotions of happiness, and everyone is out to exploit it.

Especially corporations.

In the mid-2000s, companies began using an incredibly abstract aesthetic; it was incredibly glossy, humanist, skeuomorphic. The colors were bright and contrasting. Nature was abound everywhere. It was all partially saturated. If you had a phone or TV during those times, you have seen it, hundreds of times.

And yet, that aesthetic is not nearly as famous as, say, Y2K or Weirdcore.

It's name is Frutiger Aero.

Named after Adrian Frutiger, the creator of the Frutiger typeface used in that era, and Windows Aero, the theme for the UI in Windows Vista and Windows 7, the images created from it looked beautiful.

Almost as if it were a utopia.

The aesthetic was a promise to the customers that their future could look like this.

But now, these hopes are rendered moot. What is left is a reminder of what could have been.


'''{{USERNAME}}.'''

'''''Do you wish to wander what we hoped for?'''''
'''''Very well...'''''
= '''''Level 833: "What was Promised to Us"''''' = {{Class|p1=1|p2=0|p3=0}} {{Blurimage|file=FA1.jpg|caption=Frutiger Aero.}} '''Level 833''''s environment is very abstract; the visual appearance of the level is stylistically comparable to the Frutiger Aero art aesthetic. Specifically, this landscape is bright and saturated, glossy bubbles float in the air, gigantic depictions of Terra from [[The Frontrooms]] are scattered about, and large static splashes of water are present throughout (see ''Anomalies''). The level takes the appearance of an expanse of temperate ocean, with islands of varying sizes being interspersed throughout.These islands are compact in shape, and the highest points of these isles are shaped like a combination of sinusoidal waves. [[File:FAS.mp3|right|300px|link=]] Vegetation covers the vast majority of islands. Inland, the plants consist mainly of Bermuda grass and oak trees, whereas the edges of these islands have more varied plant life, including flowers. Despite this, lifeforms (specifically from the superclass ''Osteichthyes'', order ''Laminariales'', class ''Anthozoa'', family ''Laridae'', and phylum ''Echinodermata'') exist, the vast majority of which being tropical fish. While these organisms are notably the same as their [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] counterparts, they have seemingly overcome their need for nutrients. Furthermore, no organism present within '''Level 833''' seems to be affected by any defects or ailments. These effects also have an impact of wanderers; one will no longer have any defects and will not feel hunger or thirst, on top of no longer having to eat food or drink water for the duration of their presence in the level. {{Blurimage|file=FA3.png|caption=An image of an airport in Level 833.}} A prominent aspect of '''Level 833''' are the many cities found on the islands. The architecture of these settlements is mostly modernist, with lofty skyscrapers dotting the cityscapes. These cities are in incredibly pristine condition, with the metallic surfaces and glass within being very lustrous. Navigation of islands with such cities are made simple by an efficient road network that connects almost all parts of the island together. Much like the buildings, the roads are of great quality. There is a notable lack of potholes or cracks present within the paving, and they are marked to be pedestrian/cyclist friendly. Cars and bicycles are commonplace in '''Level 833''', though older cars/bikes are more scarce than modern ones. Bus stops, docks and airports can also be found in these cities, and the planes, boats and buses within them still function, despite a clear lack of pilots or bus drivers (see ''Anomalies''). '''Level 833''' also follows a 24 hour day and night cycle and a rudimentary water cycle also exists (see ''Anomalies''). ==Anomalies== '''Level 833''' is the host to a myriad of anomalies/properties that are unique to itself ranging from image-distorting anomalies to the aforementioned static splashes and depictions of Terra; these anomalies contribute to the aesthetic fame that this level is known for. The anomalies have been listed and labelled accordingly.
A833-1
===A833-1: "Green Thumbs"=== {{Blurimage|file=FAA833-1.png|caption=A simple diagram showing how A833-1 works.}} A833-1, colloquially called the Green Thumbs, is an image-distorting anomaly that is unique among other anomalies of that sort. While other image-distorting anomalies usually change the picture in terms of saturation, noise, or other distorting factors (or simply make taking a recording or picture a mass of static as is the case with [[Level 12]]), A833-1 instead places either a sizable amount of dense foliage on a section of the image that contains vegetation or a set of low-opacity curved lines in a given point of the sky (or both). This effect does not occur if the images are devoid of vegetation, and even in the case where images contain plants, only 1 in 20 of such images become victim to A833-1. Numerous theories have been posited about why this anomaly occurs, ranging from the foliage that the anomaly shows being some sort of undocumented matter that only appears on photographs to the level itself somehow influencing the appearance of the photographs — none of which are able to be confirmed. (''The first image within this document is a prime example of A833-1 taking place'').
A833-2
===A833-2: "Waterlogged Monitors"=== {{Blurimage|file=FAA833-2.webp|caption=A photo showing an example of A833-2.}} {{Blurimage|file=Diagram2.png|caption=A diagram depicting an example of the level's water cycle, which A833-2 is involved in.|align=left}} A833-2, often called a Waterlogged Monitor, takes the form of a modern television screen/work computer monitor, which appear similar to TVs and monitors made by companies like Samsung, LG, and Lenovo — though none of their logos or word marks can be found. The screen often depicts an image that falls under the Frutiger Aero aesthetic, or otherwise it depicts some footage of a level wherein the main aspect of it is the water. Waterlogged Monitors will have a large volume of warm ocean water pouring out of the screen continuously, with no method able to prevent it; fish and birds also frequently come out of the screens, and the origin of both the water and the organisms that come out of A833-2 is unknown. Waterlogged Monitors are always found in the ocean floating in a manner that prevents the screens from being submerged in the water, though rarely the monitors are frozen in an upright position partially under the water. A833-2 is a large contributor to maintaining the water cycle of '''Level 833''', since it feeds the greatest amount of water into the ocean.
A833-3
===A833-3: "MiniTerras"=== {{Blurimage|file=FA2.jpg|caption=A photo depicting a broken version of A833-3.}} A833-3, frequently referred to as a MiniTerra has the appearance of a large sphere of either a lime green or blue color. These are either with the continents of Terra or lines of latitude and longitude, both of which are depictions of Terra. The size of MiniTerras range from a meter to several hundreds of meters in size, and roughly 75% of their volume seems to be filled with ocean water. MiniTerras seemingly evade the laws of gravity, as despite being much heavier than the air within '''Level 833''', they always float in the air. It should be noted that the shell that contains the water within a MiniTerra is fragile — the shell is made out of a material that shares a lot of properties with glass and drywall, with the material being opaque. As such, it is not strange to find broken MiniTerras pouring out the water within for up to hours on end. Broken MiniTerras also somewhat contribute to '''Level 833''''s water cycle (see ''A833-2'') by spilling their contents into the ocean, though not to such an extent as A833-2.
A833-4
===A833-4: "Eternal Globules"=== {{Blurimage|file=FA833-4.jpg|caption=A photo depicting cases of A833-4.}} A833-4 takes the appearance of a large, glossy soap bubble, and are often called Eternal Globules for this reason. The anomaly is large, with the average bubble having a radius of 10 meters — due to their relatively light mass, their movement is influenced by winds, giving Eternal Globules a movement pattern akin to Brownian motion. A833-4 shares some properties with A833-3 (specifically their spherical shape and their hollow structure), though Eternal Globules are much more fragile, transparent, and not as permanent as the other.
A833-5
===A833-5: "Splashes"=== {{Blurimage|file=FA833-5.jpg|caption=A photo showing A833-5 behind a city.}} A833-5 is an anomalous phenomenon that takes the appearance of a ginormous formation, roughly similar in size to the many cities found across '''Level 833'''. Commonly called a Splash, the phenomenon is formed by ocean water weaving itself into a massive formation reminiscent of a static splash of water. These are often found in close proximity to cities, though larger splashes can be found deeper in the ocean. It should be noted that while some splashes are permanent, others are not, and the water that forms them will slowly fall back down into the ocean, leaving no trace of its former presence. It is impossible to fully determine which cases of A833-5 are permanent and which ones are not, though a good rule of thumb to follow is that splashes that are farther away from land generally tend to last longer; this could be due to lack of human intervention with ones farther out to sea.
A833-6
===A833-6: "SenVehicles"=== {{Blurimage|file=FA833-6.jpg|caption=A photo showing a case of A833-6 taking off.}} A833-6 is an anomaly that affects all public transit vehicles, and planes; the anomaly allows these vehicles to have autonomy over where they move, despite the mechanics of these vehicles seemingly not allowing for them to be driven without the need for a driver. For this reason, cases of A833-6 are colloquially referred to as SenVehicles (short for "Sentient Vehicles"), however, their sentience has been put into question, as they seemingly have no awareness of the outside world, and will not react to environmental factors. SenVehicles function identically to their counterparts in [[The Frontrooms]], meaning one will be able to board them to be taken to other areas swiftly without much risk. The vehicles that A833-6 acts upon are ones that would be found in common use roughly from 2004 to 2013, aligning with the time period wherein the Frutiger Aero aesthetic was widely used.

==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== * Toying with computers that have the Windows XP operating system within the Rosatin Hotel of [[Level 399]] may send one here; * Noclipping in the Multi-Level Error in [[Level 99]] could send one here; * Finding a modernist home surrounded by kayaks in the waters of [[Level 7]] and entering will take one to '''Level 833'''; * Though rare, one might be able to access '''Level 833''' through a door branded with a mural of the globe within any level with long winding hallways. ===Exits=== * One can return to [[Level 399]] by experimenting with computers with modern operating systems — such computers are rare; * Wandering a wheat field will transition a wanderer to [[Level 10]]; * Swimming out for long enough may lead you to [[Level 100]]'s beach. {{Underline|00FFFF}}
